name: Hawa Mahal
description: Hawa Mahal, the Palace of Winds, is a stunning five-story palace built from red and pink sandstone. Constructed in 1799, it features 953 small windows called jharokhas, designed to let royal women observe street festivals without being seen. It remains one of the most photographed monuments in Jaipur and a defining symbol of the Pink City.
highlights: Best photographed in early morning light
Small museum inside with royal artifacts
Rooftop view of the City Palace and Jantar Mantar

reviewsList.name: Ananya R.
reviewsList.text: Absolutely breathtaking architecture, best visited early morning to avoid crowds and get great photos.

reviewsList.name: Rohit S.
reviewsList.text: Beautiful monument, the inside is smaller than expected but the facade alone is worth the visit.

reviewsList.name: Meera K.
reviewsList.text: A must-visit in Jaipur. The view from the top windows over the city is stunning.
